title: Clone Frame.io in 30 Minutes with Claude Code
description: Build a custom video commenting tool that replaces expensive SaaS subscriptions using Claude Code and vibe coding.

cards.title: Clone a Billion Dollar App
cards.body: You'll learn to recreate Frame.io's core features using Claude Code in just 30 minutes. We'll build a custom video commenting tool that costs nothing and does exactly what you need.

cards.title: The SaaS Problem
cards.body: Most teams pay thousands yearly for tools like Frame.io but only use 20% of the features. The solution? Build exactly what you need, skip what you don't.

analogy.text: It's like paying for a whole pizza when you only eat two slices

bullets: Most users only need video upload + timestamped comments
Custom tools can replace expensive subscriptions

cards.title: Map Your Essential Features
cards.body: Before coding, list every feature you actually use day-to-day. Ignore everything else — this becomes your prompt foundation.
bullets: Upload and save video as project
cards.safe: Focus only on features you use weekly — you can always add more later

cards.title: What is Claude Code?
cards.body: Claude Code is an AI agent that can browse the internet, write code, and deploy apps — all in one interface. It's not just an API wrapper, it's the full coding environment.

concept.title: Vibe Code vs Other Platforms
items: Lovable: Custom AI for web apps
Vibe Code: Runs actual Claude Code in browser

cards.title: Set Up Your Development Environment
cards.body: Open your preferred AI coding platform. We'll use Vibe Code, but you can also use Replit, Lovable, or direct Claude access.
bullets: Go to vibecode.dev (or your preferred platform)
Click 'Web App' to start a new project
Select Claude Opus as your AI model

helpItems.title: Alternative Platforms
helpItems.content: Replit Agent, Lovable, or Claude.ai with Artifacts all work for this tutorial

cards.title: Quick Check
cards.question: Why should you focus only on features you actually use when building internal tools?

options.text: It makes the app look simpler
It reduces development time and creates exactly what you need
It's easier to maintain complex features later

cards.correctFeedback: Exactly! Build only what you need to avoid complexity and save time.
cards.wrongFeedback: Focus on essential features to create tools that actually solve your problems efficiently.

cards.title: Craft Your AI Prompt
cards.body: Write a detailed prompt that includes feature requirements, security, and context about the original tool.
cards.code:
Build an app like Frame.io with these features:
1. Upload video, save as project
2. Add timestamped comments while watching
3. Attach files to comments
4. Password protect with 'banana'
5. Generate shareable public links

Search internet for context on Frame.io layout
cards.codeCaption: Include 'search internet for context' to help AI understand the original tool's design
cards.warn: Always add password protection for internal tools instead of building full authentication

cards.title: Launch the Build
cards.body: Paste your prompt into the AI coding platform and watch Claude Code break down the project into tasks.

source.author: Riley Brown
source.description: Watching Claude Code create the project structure and task list

bullets: Hit 'Generate' and watch the magic happen

cards.title: First Build Complete!
cards.body: Claude Code just created your entire app structure, database, and frontend in minutes. Time to test what we've got and refine it.

cards.title: Test and Identify Issues
cards.body: Test your new app immediately. Don't expect perfection — identify what needs fixing and communicate clearly with the AI.
bullets: Enter your password to access the app
Check if comments work at specific timestamps

troubleshoot.issue: App asks for video URL instead of file upload
troubleshoot.solution: Tell AI: 'I want file upload, not URL input'

troubleshoot.issue: Comments don't save to specific timestamps
troubleshoot.solution: Specify: 'Comments should capture current video time when created'

cards.title: Iterative Improvement
cards.body: The key to successful vibe coding is conversational debugging. Describe problems naturally instead of rewriting entire prompts.

analogy.text: Talk to the AI like you'd explain a problem to a colleague — be specific but natural

bullets: 'I don't want a URL input, I want a file upload button'
cards.safe: Small iterative changes work better than complete rewrites

cards.title: Fix the File Upload
cards.body: Address the most critical issue first — proper file upload instead of URL input.
cards.code: I don't want a video URL input. I want users to upload a video file directly. Please fix the upload to accept actual video files and store them properly.

source.author: Riley Brown
source.description: Fixing the file upload functionality with natural language

cards.title: Test Video Upload and Comments
cards.body: Upload a test video and verify the core functionality — timestamped comments and file attachments.
bullets: Play the video and pause at different points

helpItems.title: Testing Tips
helpItems.content: Use a short video (under 2 minutes) for faster testing iterations

cards.title: Understanding Vibe Coding
cards.question: What's the best approach when your AI-built app has issues?

options.text: Rewrite the entire prompt from scratch
Start over with a different AI tool
Try to fix the code manually

cards.correctFeedback: Perfect! Conversational debugging is the key to effective vibe coding.
cards.wrongFeedback: Remember: describe problems naturally and let the AI iterate on the existing code.

cards.title: Secure Your Internal Tool
cards.body: Change the default password and prepare your app for team sharing. For internal tools, simple password protection often beats complex authentication.
cards.code: Change the password from 'banana' to a secure password for my team. Also make sure the shared project links work properly for view-only access.
cards.warn: Never leave default passwords in production, even for internal tools

cards.title: Add Custom Features
cards.body: Now that core functionality works, add features that Frame.io doesn't have — this is where custom tools shine.

cards.title: Deploy and Share
cards.body: Your app is ready for your team! Deploy it and start saving money on expensive SaaS subscriptions.
bullets: Send the app URL to your team
Train your team on the new workflow
cards.safe: Most vibe coding platforms handle deployment automatically — no server management needed

cards.title: The Business Impact
cards.body: You've just saved thousands of dollars annually and built exactly what your team needs. This is the power of vibe coding for business.
bullets: Built only needed features, no bloat

analogy.text: Like having a custom suit instead of off-the-rack — perfect fit, better price

cards.title: You're Now a Vibe Coder!
cards.body: Congratulations! You've cloned a billion-dollar app in 30 minutes. You can now build custom internal tools for any business need, replacing expensive SaaS subscriptions with exactly what your team requires.
